newsapp package¶

newsapp.apps module¶

Application configuration for the News 24/7 app.

class newsapp.apps.NewsappConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

Configure the Django app and load signal handlers on startup.

ready() → None[source]¶

Import signal handlers when the application starts.

newsapp.admin module¶

Admin registrations for News 24/7 models.

class newsapp.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Customize admin display for application users.

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for publishers.

class newsapp.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for news articles.

class newsapp.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for newsletters.

class newsapp.admin.ApprovedArticleLogAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for approval log entries.

newsapp.forms module¶

Form classes used by the News 24/7 web interface.

class newsapp.forms.LoginForm(request=None, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AuthenticationForm

Authenticate a user from the login page.

The ‘request’ parameter is set for custom auth use by subclasses. The form data comes in via the standard ‘data’ kwarg.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class newsapp.forms.RegisterForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

Register a new user with an email address and role selection.

Apply Bootstrap classes to the default user creation fields.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class newsapp.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Create or edit a news article from the web interface.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class newsapp.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Create or edit a newsletter and attach selected articles.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

newsapp.models module¶

Database models for the News 24/7 application.

This module defines the custom user model, publisher records, articles, newsletters, and the approval log used by the API workflow.

class newsapp.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Application user with role-based access and optional subscriptions.

clean() → None[source]¶

Validate that only reader accounts can keep publisher and journalist subscriptions.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Persist the user and clear role-incompatible relationship fields.

The brief asks for reader-only subscription fields to be effectively absent for journalists and journalist-specific publication relations to be absent for readers. In Django, reverse relations cannot literally be set to None, so this method keeps the stored data equivalent by clearing reader subscriptions for all non-reader roles. The journalist publication fields remain reverse relations and naturally resolve to empty querysets for non-journalists.

property is_reader: bool¶

Return True when the user is assigned to the reader role.

property is_editor: bool¶

Return True when the user is assigned to the editor role.

property is_journalist: bool¶

Return True when the user is assigned to the journalist role.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class newsapp.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

News publisher with assigned editors and journalists.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class newsapp.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

News article submitted by a journalist and optionally tied to a publisher.

clean() → None[source]¶

Validate that each article is authored by a user with the journalist role.

mark_approved(editor: CustomUser) → None[source]¶

Approve the article and store approval metadata.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

Bases: Model

Curated collection of articles created by journalists or editors.

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

newsapp.permissions module¶

Custom permission helpers used by the web and API layers.

newsapp.permissions.user_in_group(user, group_name: str) → bool[source]¶

Return whether the authenticated user belongs to the named group.

newsapp.permissions.is_effective_editor(user) → bool[source]¶

Return whether a user should be treated as an editor.

newsapp.permissions.is_effective_journalist(user) → bool[source]¶

Return whether a user should be treated as a journalist.

class newsapp.permissions.IsJournalist[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to effective journalists.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True when the request user is a journalist.

class newsapp.permissions.IsEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to effective editors.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True when the request user is an editor.

class newsapp.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to editors and journalists.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True when the request user is an editor or journalist.

newsapp.serializers module¶

Serializers for the News 24/7 REST API.

class newsapp.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Represent a user in API responses.

class newsapp.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Represent a publisher in API responses.

class newsapp.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Validate and serialize article data for list, detail, and create views.

class newsapp.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize newsletter records and their linked articles.

class newsapp.serializers.ApprovedArticleLogSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize the internal approved-article log records.

newsapp.signals module¶

Signal handlers and startup helpers for the News 24/7 application.

This module keeps Django groups in sync with application roles, assigns permissions after migrations, emails subscribers when an article is approved, and posts approved-article events to the internal API log endpoint.

newsapp.signals.create_groups_and_permissions(sender, **kwargs) → None[source]¶

Create groups and refresh role permissions after migrations complete.

newsapp.signals.assign_group_by_role(sender, instance, created, **kwargs) → None[source]¶

Keep the user’s Django group aligned with the selected application role.

Parameters:

  • sender – The user model class that fired the signal.

  • instance – The saved user instance.

  • created – Indicates whether the user was created in this save call.

Returns:

None

newsapp.signals.notify_on_article_approval(sender, instance: Article, created: bool, **kwargs) → None[source]¶

Send notifications and create an internal log when an article is approved.

Parameters:

  • sender – The model class that fired the signal.

  • instance – The saved article instance.

  • created – Indicates whether the article was newly created.

Returns:

None

newsapp.urls module¶

URL routes for the News 24/7 web pages and API endpoints.

newsapp.views module¶

Views for the News 24/7 web interface and REST API.

class newsapp.views.HomeView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginView

Render the login page and redirect authenticated users to the dashboard.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

authentication_form¶

alias of LoginForm

class newsapp.views.AppLogoutView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LogoutView

Support logout requests from both GET links and POST forms.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

get(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Allow logout from the navbar link as well as POST submissions.

newsapp.views.register_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Allow users to create an account and get auto-assigned to the right group.

newsapp.views.dashboard_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Display featured articles, newsletters, and editor review items.

newsapp.views.article_list_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Show the list of approved articles available to signed-in users.

newsapp.views.article_detail_view(request: HttpRequest, pk: int) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Display a single article when the current user is allowed to view it.

newsapp.views.article_create_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Create a new article from the web interface.

newsapp.views.article_update_view(request: HttpRequest, pk: int) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Edit an existing article while enforcing role-based restrictions.

newsapp.views.article_delete_view(request: HttpRequest, pk: int) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Delete an article when the current user has permission.

newsapp.views.review_articles_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

List pending articles for editors to review.

newsapp.views.approve_article_view(request: HttpRequest, pk: int) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Approve a pending article from the web interface.

newsapp.views.newsletter_list_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Show all newsletters with their linked articles and authors.

newsapp.views.newsletter_create_view(request: HttpRequest) → HttpResponse[source]¶

Create a newsletter from selected articles.

class newsapp.views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

List approved articles or create a new article via the API.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

get_queryset()[source]¶

Return approved articles with related author and publisher data preloaded.

get_permissions()[source]¶

Allow authenticated reads and restrict article creation to journalists.

perform_create(serializer)[source]¶

Create a new article and attach the currently authenticated author.

class newsapp.views.SubscribedArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

Return approved articles from subscribed publishers and journalists.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

get_queryset()[source]¶

Build a queryset limited to approved articles from the user’s subscriptions.

class newsapp.views.ArticleRetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve, update, or delete a single article through the API.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

get_object()[source]¶

Return the selected article only when the current user may view it.

update(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Update an article while preserving the journalist re-approval workflow.

destroy(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Delete an article when the current user is authorised to remove it.

class newsapp.views.ApproveArticleAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: APIView

Approve an article through a dedicated API endpoint.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

post(request, pk: int, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Mark the selected article as approved and return the updated payload.

class newsapp.views.NewsletterListAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

List newsletters that can be accessed by authenticated users.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class newsapp.views.ApprovedArticleLogCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: CreateAPIView

Create or update approved article log entries for downstream integrations.

Constructor. Called in the URLconf; can contain helpful extra keyword arguments, and other things.

serializer_class¶

alias of ApprovedArticleLogSerializer

create(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a log entry or update the existing one for the same article.
